senses.definition: to gradually learn to accept or deal with something unpleasant

examples.x: It took Jane a while to come to terms with her divorce, but eventually, she found happiness again.
Tom struggled to come to terms with his diagnosis, but with time, he learned to manage his condition.
The company had to come to terms with the reality of a shrinking market and adapt their business strategy.
Coming to terms with the loss of a beloved pet can be a deeply emotional experience.
Mary needed to come to terms with her past mistakes in order to move forward and build a better future.

senses.definition: to accept or tolerate a difficult or undesirable situation that one cannot change without complaint

examples.x: When faced with a long and boring meeting, Tom tried to grin and bear it to maintain professionalism.
The students had to grin and bear the strict exam regulations to succeed in their studies.
In a difficult family situation, she had to grin and bear the tension during the holiday gatherings.

senses.definition: an unpleasant thing that requires acceptance in order for a certain thing to be achieved

examples.x: Many people see paying taxes as a necessary evil to fund essential public services.
Implementing strict security measures at airports is considered a necessary evil to ensure passenger safety.
For some, taking medication daily becomes a necessary evil to manage a chronic health condition.
Some argue that surveillance in the interest of national security is a necessary evil in the modern world.
In the world of business, some may view layoffs as a necessary evil when a company faces financial difficulties.

senses.definition: to not try to interfere or make changes in a situation or with something that is already functioning satisfactorily to avoid causing unnecessary problems

examples.x: To be honest, you're better off letting well alone at this point.
The garden is thriving as it is; it's best to leave well alone and not overwater the plants.
The old computer is slow but still working; it's probably best to let well alone until we absolutely need to replace it.
The company's policies have been effective for years, so it's wise to let well alone and not introduce unnecessary changes.
She had a unique decorating style that everyone loved, so they decided to leave well alone and not redecorate the room.

senses.definition: to avoid getting involved with a person or thing or stop bothering them

examples.x: A place that lets them be for whatever period they choose.
There's so much power in just letting things be.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ ɹˈoʊl ˈɔf wˈʌnz bˈæk

senses.definition: to not allow negative things such as an unfair criticism, etc. to have any effect on one

examples.x: So I've tried to adapt, letting the comments roll off my back.
Despite the harsh critique of his artwork, he learned to let it roll off his back and continue pursuing his passion.
As a public figure, dealing with constant scrutiny is essential, so politicians often develop the skill to let criticism roll off their backs.

senses.definition: said to a person after they have an unpleasant or sad experience to encourage them to keep on living as they used to

examples.x: Despite the political upheaval, for most people life goes on as usual.
After her divorce, Jane realized that life goes on, and she started building a new, fulfilling life for herself.
Despite the loss of their business in the economic downturn, they found the strength to carry on, knowing that life goes on.
Coping with the loss of a loved one is never easy, but the understanding that life goes on helps people find a way to heal and move forward.
Even in the face of adversity, John remained optimistic, knowing that life goes on and better days will come.

senses.definition: something that is finally dealt with and does not bother one anymore

examples.x: Finding her lost passport was a load off her mind; she was worried about her upcoming trip.
Paying off the last installment of their student loans was a weight off their minds, allowing them to plan for the future with more freedom.
After a thorough medical examination, the doctor's reassurance that everything was fine was a significant load off his mind.
Getting that promotion at work was a weight off his mind, as it meant he could provide better for his family.
The successful recovery of their stolen car was a load off their minds, and they could finally relax and enjoy their vacation.

senses.definition: to regain control of oneself after being in a bad mood or emotional state
to accept unpleasant or difficult things or situations, such as criticism or misfortune, without complaining

examples.x: Despite the harsh feedback from the client, he took the criticism on the chin and used it to improve his work.
After losing the championship, the athlete took the defeat on the chin and vowed to train harder for the next competition.
Facing financial setbacks, they decided to take it on the chin and work diligently to get back on their feet.
When her project failed, she took the failure on the chin, learned from her mistakes, and began a new venture.
In the face of personal tragedy, he took it on the chin and channeled his grief into a meaningful cause to help others.

word_list.word: that is the way the cookie crumbles (in the market|)
word_list.pronunciation: ðæt ɪz ðə wˈeɪ ðə kˈʊki kɹˈʌmbəlz ɪnðə mˈɑːɹkɪt ɔːɹ

senses.definition: said after an unfortunate event to mean one must accept the situation as it is

examples.x: John lost the tennis match, but he just shrugged and said, "That's the way the cookie crumbles."
The company's stocks plummeted despite their efforts to boost them; the CEO acknowledged, "That's the way the cookie crumbles in the market."

senses.definition: a peaceful time when things has improved following a period of difficulty, stress, or chaos

examples.x: After the heated argument, there was the calm after the storm, and the couple finally had a productive conversation.
The financial crisis took a toll on the company, but they were optimistic about the calm after the storm, hoping for a business rebound.
The political unrest eventually subsided, and the nation experienced the calm after the storm, with a return to stability.
Following the intense preparations for the event, there was the calm after the storm, and everyone could relax and enjoy the occasion.
In the aftermath of a challenging project, the team looked forward to the calm after the storm, which allowed them to reflect and regroup.

word_list.pronunciation: ðə kˈɑːm lˈʌl bɪfˌoːɹ ðɪ ɐnˈʌðɚ stˈoːɹm

senses.definition: a time when everything seems peaceful or fine before serious problems arise

examples.x: The peaceful negotiations seemed like the calm before another storm of disputes and disagreements among the nations.
Following the calm before the storm of intense preparations, the team was ready to face the challenges of the upcoming competition.

senses.definition: a situation that is an absolute disaster or is the worst thing that could possibly happen

examples.x: Failing one exam is not the end of the world.
Losing his job felt like the end of the world to him, as he had no savings and bills piling up.
When her phone fell into the water, she acted like it was the end of the world, even though it could be fixed.
Failing the first exam in a course may seem like the end of the world, but there are opportunities to improve.
Some people treat minor inconveniences as if they were the end of the world, causing unnecessary stress.

senses.definition: used for saying that one must continue an activity, performance, etc. despite any problem or difficulty

examples.x: Despite feeling unwell, the lead actor insisted that the show must go on and delivered a remarkable performance.
The power outage caused a disruption, but the event organizers quickly arranged alternative arrangements, ensuring that the show must go on.
The band's guitarist broke a string during the concert, but they continued to play without missing a beat, knowing that the show must go on.
Despite facing financial difficulties, the theater company rallied together, determined that the show must go on for the sake of their artistic vision.
The speaker lost their notes moments before going on stage, but they improvised and confidently delivered their speech, understanding that the show must go on.

word_list.pronunciation: ðæt ɪz ðə wˈeɪ ðə bˈɔːl bˈaʊns

senses.definition: used to say that one cannot control everything as bad things happen regardless
to accept the fact that one has been defeated

examples.x: Jim beat me for the fourth time, so I threw in the towel.
After hours of trying to fix the broken computer, he finally decided to throw in the towel and call a professional.
The team faced numerous obstacles during the project, but they were determined not to throw in the towel.
She was determined to complete the marathon despite the pain, refusing to throw in the towel until she crossed the finish line.
When the negotiations reached an impasse, both parties agreed to throw in the towel and consider alternative solutions.

word_list.word: (put|stick) that in {one's} pipe and smoke it
word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt stˈɪk ðæt ɪn wˈʌnz pˈaɪp ænd smˈoʊk ɪt

senses.definition: used for telling someone to accept and consider something as true or valid, usually after a disagreement or argument

examples.x: After the heated debate, he declared, "I won the argument, so you can put that in your pipe and smoke it!"
In response to skepticism about his business plan, he confidently stated, "I've done my homework, so you can put that in your pipe and smoke it!"
The coach, frustrated by the doubters, exclaimed, "We're going to win this game, so stick that in your pipe and smoke it!"

senses.definition: to experience life in the present moment and focus less on long-term plans or financial preparations

examples.x: Instead of stressing about the future, Jane prefers to live from day to day and enjoy the present.
After retiring, he decided to live from day to day, traveling and savoring the simple joys of life.
In times of hardship, it's often best to focus on living from day to day and finding happiness in small moments.
She realized that worrying about tomorrow wasn't helping, so she decided to live from day to day and reduce her stress.
While some people plan far ahead, others are content to live from day to day, finding contentment in the here and now.
